description: Home IDS — Full threat, anomaly, malware and geo-risk dashboard

panels.title: Risk Score Timeline — Per Device
panels.description: Time-series risk for every device. Threshold lines at 6 (alert) and 8 (critical).

panels.title: DNS Query Rate / min — Per Device
Z-Scores — Anomaly Detector (Query Rate, NXDOMAIN, Blocked)
panels.description: z-score > 3 = statistically anomalous for that device. Now includes NXDOMAIN and Blocked ratio z-scores. New: even a device with 2% NXDOMAIN baseline shows a spike if it suddenly hits 15%.

panels.title: Blocked DNS Ratio
panels.description: Ratio of DNS queries blocked by Pi-hole per device. High ratio may indicate malware trying known-bad domains.

panels.title: NXDOMAIN Ratio — DGA / Malware Indicator
panels.description: DGA malware generates many non-existent domains. Ratio > 0.3 is suspicious. > 0.5 is high confidence malware.

alert.message: NXDOMAIN ratio exceeded 0.3 — possible DGA malware
alert.name: NXDOMAIN Spike Alert

panels.title: Suspicious / DGA-Like Domains — Count
panels.description: Count of domains flagged by the DGA heuristic (high entropy label, low vowel ratio, len > 18). Each dot is a potential malware domain.

alert.message: DGA suspicious domain count > 5 — possible malware C2
alert.name: DGA Domain Alert

panels.title: DNS Entropy — Per Device
panels.description: Average Shannon entropy of queried domain labels. Normal browsing: 2.5–3.2. DGA malware: > 3.7. Encrypted/tunnelled DNS: > 4.0.

panels.title: ML Anomaly Score — IsolationForest
panels.description: Scores > 0 indicate anomalous behaviour. > 0.3 = suspicious. > 0.5 = strong anomaly. Score is learned per-device — same score means different things for a phone vs a NAS.

alert.message: ML anomaly score exceeded 0.4 — IsolationForest flagging unusual behaviour
alert.name: ML Anomaly Alert

panels.title: All Devices — Current Status
panels.description: Full per-device snapshot. Sorted by Risk score. Rows colour by threshold.

panels.title: 🔬 IDS Script Self-Test — Is It Actually Working?
Scoring Engine Output (risk > 0 = working)
panels.description: If 0.0 the scorer has no data yet or baselines are cold. Any non-zero value confirms the risk engine is running. ML score stays 0.0 until 5,000 samples per device are collected (~21 min at 8 devices).

panels.title: ML Engine Output (non-zero = working)
panels.description: IsolationForest score. Will be 0.0 until warmup (5000 samples). Any non-zero value confirms ML scoring is running.

panels.title: Events Ingested/min — Poll Loop Live
panels.description: Rate of DNS events ingested from Pi-hole. 0 means the poll loop has stalled or Pi-hole has no new queries.

panels.title: Alerts Fired — Pipeline End-to-End
panels.description: Alert rate over time. Flat zero on a busy network = threshold too high or pipeline broken.

panels.title: Max Risk Score by Device Type
panels.description: Worst current risk score per device category. Helps spot an entire device class being targeted (e.g. all IoT devices beaconing).

panels.title: Average NXDOMAIN Ratio by Device Type
panels.description: IoT and phone device types with high NXDOMAIN ratios are strong malware indicators — these devices have limited legitimate reasons to query non-existent domains.

panels.title: 🌍 Global Threat Map — Risk by Destination
Destination Risk Map — Bubble = Query Volume, Colour = Risk
panels.description: Each bubble is a destination seen in DNS traffic. Size = query volume. Colour = risk score. Requires GeoIP lat/lon labels. Hover for country, city, ASN.

layers.name: Destination Risk Map — Bubble = Query Volume, Colour = Risk

panels.title: Threat Density by Country
panels.description: Countries shaded by cumulative risk score from their infrastructure. Red = high ongoing threat.

panels.title: DNS Query Rate by Location — Bubble Size = Queries/min
panels.description: Where your DNS queries are going. Large bubbles = high query rate. Orange/red = unusually high traffic to that location.

layers.name: DNS Query Rate by Location — Bubble Size = Queries/min

panels.title: Total DNS Traffic by Country
panels.description: Cumulative DNS query count shaded by country. Unexpected high-traffic countries warrant investigation.

panels.title: Beaconing Destinations — Countries Receiving C2 Traffic
panels.description: Countries shaded by total beaconing detections. Any non-expected country lit up here is a potential C2 channel.

layers.name: Beaconing Destinations — Countries Receiving C2 Traffic

panels.title: Beaconing Rate — Timeline by Country/ASN
panels.description: Beaconing detections per 5 min. A line that never drops to zero = persistent C2 keep-alive. Alert fires at > 5 events per 5 min.

alert.message: Beaconing to foreign destination — possible C2
alert.name: Beaconing C2 Alert

panels.title: DNS Entropy by Country — DGA & Tunnelling Risk
panels.description: Countries shaded by avg DNS query entropy. High entropy to specific countries = DGA malware or DNS tunnelling exfiltration. Normal: 2.5–3.2. Suspicious: > 3.7.

layers.name: DNS Entropy by Country — DGA & Tunnelling Risk

panels.title: Unique Domain Count by Country
panels.description: Countries shaded by unique domain count. High unique domains to unusual countries = C2 domain rotation or exfiltration probing.

panels.title: Threat Hit Density by Country
panels.description: Countries shaded by cumulative threat hits — DNS queries that triggered risk scoring. Sustained red = active threat source country.

panels.title: Device Reach — How Many Devices Communicate with Each Country
panels.description: Countries shaded by number of your devices talking to them. An unexpected country with many devices = potential mass compromise or supply-chain C2.

layers.name: Device Reach — How Many Devices Communicate with Each Country

panels.title: Threat Hits Timeline — Rate per Country
panels.description: Rate of high-risk DNS queries per country over time. A country that suddenly appears and stays is an active campaign.

panels.title: Risk Velocity — Z-Score of Risk vs Device Baseline
panels.description: How fast the risk score is rising relative to that device's normal risk level. A device going from 0→7 in one cycle shows high velocity even if 7 isn't a record. Z-score > 3 = sudden spike. More alarming than a stable high score.

panels.title: 🆕 New Domain Burst & DNS Tunnelling Detection
New Domains / Window — First-Seen Domain Rate
panels.description: Domains appearing for the first time ever from this device. DGA generates hundreds of never-before-seen domains per cycle. Normal browsing revisits the same CDNs. > 20 new domains in 5 min = DGA burst signal.

panels.title: Deep DNS Domains — Label Count > 5 (Tunnelling Indicator)
panels.description: Domains with more than 5 DNS labels: a.b.c.d.e.attacker.com. Legitimate domains rarely exceed 4 labels. DNS tunnelling encodes exfiltrated data as subdomains. Any non-zero value here warrants immediate investigation.

panels.title: 🎯 DGA Family Fingerprint — NXDOMAIN TLD Concentration
NXDOMAIN TLD Concentration — DGA Family Signal
panels.description: Fraction of all DNS traffic going to the single most-queried TLD. DGA families generate all variants under one TLD (.ru, .xyz, .cc). Combined with high NXDOMAIN ratio: > 0.7 = near-certain DGA family pattern. Legitimate browsing distributes across .com .net .org .io etc.

panels.title: TLD Concentration × NXDOMAIN — Combined DGA Score
panels.description: Product of TLD concentration and NXDOMAIN ratio. Both high simultaneously = strong DGA family fingerprint. Alert fires when product > 0.2 (e.g. 0.7 conc × 0.3 nx).

alert.message: DGA family fingerprint: high TLD concentration × NXDOMAIN ratio
alert.name: DGA Family Alert

panels.title: NXDOMAIN Ratio Z-Score — Per-Device Baseline Deviation
panels.description: How anomalous the current NXDOMAIN ratio is vs this device's own history. A device with 0% baseline hitting 3% shows z=15 even though 3% is below the absolute 30% threshold. Per-device baselines catch low-and-slow DGA.

panels.title: Blocked Ratio Z-Score — Sudden Pi-Hole Block Spike
panels.description: Z-score of blocked DNS ratio vs device baseline. A device that normally has 0% blocked suddenly hitting 10% blocked means it's trying to reach domains Pi-hole knows are malicious.

panels.title: DGA Domain Count Z-Score — Suspicious Domain Burst
panels.description: Z-score of suspicious/DGA-like domain count vs device baseline. Even 2 DGA domains from a device that normally sees 0 shows a high z-score, catching early-stage infections before the absolute count threshold fires.

panels.title: TI Risk Score — Live IOC Match Contribution
panels.description: Direct match against Feodo/URLhaus/ThreatFox/OTX/AbuseIPDB/VirusTotal. Any non-zero value means a domain or IP from this device is in a live TI feed. Value = confidence × 4.0. Bypasses the normal 2-signal gate.

panels.title: Active TI Matches — Devices with Known IOC
panels.description: Count of devices currently matching a threat intel feed.

panels.title: TI IOC Hits — Rate by Feed Source
panels.description: Cumulative IOC hits per feed. Feodo=botnet C2, URLhaus=malware domains, AbuseIPDB=reported IPs, VT=cross-vendor validation.

panels.title: 🕸️ Zeek Network Signals — Direct IP & TLS Detection
🔴 Malicious JA3 TLS Fingerprint — Known Malware TLS Stack
panels.description: JA3 fingerprint matches known-bad TLS implementations: Cobalt Strike, Metasploit, Dridex, Trickbot, Sliver C2, Havoc C2. Any non-zero value here is near-certain malware. Investigate immediately.

alert.message: CRITICAL: Malicious JA3 TLS fingerprint detected — known malware C2 stack
alert.name: Malicious JA3 Alert

panels.title: Suspicious Port Connections (4444, 31337, IRC etc)
panels.description: Outbound connections to ports commonly used by malware: 4444/4445 (Metasploit), 31337 (Elite backdoor), 6667/6697 (IRC C2), 1080 (SOCKS proxy). Any sustained traffic here warrants investigation.

panels.title: Direct IP Connections — Unique Destinations (Zeek conn.log)
panels.description: Unique destination IPs contacted directly without DNS lookup. Malware often hard-codes C2 server IPs to bypass DNS-based detection. Pi-hole cannot see these — only Zeek can.

panels.title: Zeek Protocol Notices & Anomalies
panels.description: Events from Zeek's notice.log and weird.log — Zeek's own protocol anomaly detection running in parallel with the IDS. Includes: scan detection, invalid certificates, protocol violations.

panels.title: Safe-Listed Devices — Excluded from Scoring
panels.description: Devices in the safe_ips list (config.json) are excluded at the Pi-hole SQL query level — they never enter the scoring loop. Pi-hole server and router must be here. safe_device=1 means the device is excluded. If you see Pi-hole or your router in the risk panels, add their IP to safe_ips.

title: Home IDS — Network Threat & Anomaly Detection
